title: Oxygenated 2-d materials
abstract: This invention relates to a method of producing oxygenated inorganic two-dimensional materials which are suitable for suspending in aqueous media. In particular, it relates to an electrochemical method of making oxygenated nanoplatelets of hexagonal boron nitride and transition metal dichalcogenides.
claims: Claims 1. A method of producing oxygenated nanoplatelets of an inorganic material from a layered inorganic material in an electrochemical cell, the layered inorganic material being formed of two or more elements that are not oxygen; the cell comprising: an inert anode which is in contact with the layered inorganic material; a cathode; an electrolyte comprising a source of polyatomic anions; and wherein the method comprises: passing a current through the cell to produce oxygenated nanoplatelets of the inorganic material. 2. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the step of passing the current through the cell produces a mixture of oxygenated nanoplatelets of the inorganic material and layered inorganic material having polyatomic anions intercalated between the layers and the method further comprises: collecting the resulting mixture of oxygenated nanoplatelets of the inorganic material and layered inorganic material having intercalated polyatomic anions; suspending the mixture in an aqueous medium to form a suspension; and subjecting the suspension to energy to exfoliate the oxygenated layered inorganic material having intercalated polyatomic anions and to increase the amount of oxygenated nanoplatelets of the inorganic material in the suspension. 3. A method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the layered inorganic material is hexagonal boron nitride. 4. A method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the layered inorganic material is a transition metal dichalcogenide. 5. A method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the inert anode is coated with the layered inorganic material. 6. A method of claim 5, wherein the entire surface of the inert anode is coated with the layered inorganic material. 7. A method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the inert anode and the layered inorganic material are together contained within a porous material. 8. A method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the inert anode is or comprises platinum. 9. A method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the electrochemical cell comprises a reference electrode. 10. A method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the electrolyte is aqueous 1 1. A method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the polyatomic anions comprise oxygen; optionally wherein the polyatomic anions are selected from nitrate, phosphate, sulfate, hydroxide, permanganate, dichromate, peroxide, percarbonate, persulfate, periodate and perchlorate. 12. A method of claim 1 1 , wherein the source of polyatomic anions is nitric acid or a nitrate salt. 13. A method of claim 11 or claim 12, wherein the electrolyte further comprises either non-ionic organic compounds or organic anions 14. A method of claim 13, wherein the non-ionic organic compounds or organic anions are carboxylic acids or carboxylate salts. 15. A method of claim 14, wherein the organic anions comprises carboxylate ions 16. A method of claim 15, wherein the carboxylate anions are citrate and/or acetate ions 17. A method of claim 16, wherein the electrolyte comprises sodium acetate and sodium citrate. 18. Oxygenated nanoplatelets of an inorganic material prepared according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 17. 19. An aqueous suspension of oxygenated nanoplatelets of hexagonal boron nitride in which the concentration of the nanoplatelets in the suspension is higher than or equal to 0.4 mg/mL. 20. Oxygenated nanoplatelets of a transition metal dichalcogenide.
